Petition For Order Approving Compensation Of Attorneys And Costs
Off Calendar Aug-11-2009 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Elvira J. Orly for James Johns. Nancy Rasch for Michael Johns. Andrew Schultz for Tom Lucas (Successor Trustee). Stephen Johns in propria persona.

Ms. Orly stated that everything is before the Court and that the unwritten agreement was that James would take care of his mother and live across the street rent free. The Court inquired as to the age of the Conservatee. Stephen Johns stated that his Mother was 81 years old. Ms. Rasch stated that the dispute is that Ms. Orly's client wanted his Mother placed and the other Brother's wanted to keep her at home. In addition, Ms. Rasch stated that the Conservatee was successfully being taken care of at home by Michael and his Wife, that Michael is not being paid yet, that the Conservatee attends an adult day program, that this should not be a burden on the Estate, that each party should bear their own fees, and that the fees are unconscionable. Stephen Johns stated that this didn't benefit his Mother and the Estate shouldn't have to pay. Ms. Rasch stated that James received free rent for 17 years with the understanding he would continue on with the care of his Mother. In addition, Ms. Rasch again stated that each party should bear their own costs. The matter was taken under submission. The Court stated that the Estate should be used for the Conservatee's benefit.

Judge: Raymond J. Arata Jr. Reporter: Arlene Howell, CSR#2542. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Bailiff: Deputy J. Gomez.

Off Calendar Jul-22-2009 Continued To Sep-02-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Lawrence Siracusa for Thomas Lucas (Trustee). Stephen Johns in Propria Persona.

Mr. Johns stated that there had been a significant change in the last week and that his brother was requesting $5,000.00 a month for attending fees. Mr. Siracusa stated that the reason 83 Sylvan Street was not being sold was that Ms. Johns resided in that property, that James Johns was in court and that he wanted to sell his interest, and that he believed that everyone would cooperate with the sale. In addition, Mr. Siracusa stated that if the sale fails, he would pursue the reverse mortgage. The Court suggested listing 84 Sylvan and stated that if there was no interest within two weeks that the parties come back to court. The Petition For Authority To Obtain Reverse Mortgage was continued to September 2, 2009. The Court granted the petition For Authority To List And Sell Real Property, signing the order for the same in open court deleting the portion authorizing the Trustee to obtain a reverse mortgage. The Court suggested the Parties work out the care negotiations.

Judge: David L. Ballati. Reporter: Helen Sorep-Matley, CSR 225. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy F. Smith.

Petition For Authority To Obtain Reverse Mortgage Secured By Conservatees Residence And To Fix Trustees Bons
Off Calendar Jul-01-2009 Continued To Jul-22-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Lawrence Siracusa for Thomas Lucas (Trustee). Stephen Johns in Propria Persona (Objector).

Mr. Johns stated that they had applied for V.A. benefits, that it would be wise to see what amount of benefits they would receive, that his Mother would not qualify for those benefits if the house was sold and that the Brothers would advance the shortfall of funds. Mr. Siracusa stated that the drafted stipulation was to sell 84 Sylvan, approve a reverse mortgage but not act on it, that the family did not get along and that there was concern about not having enough money to meet respite care. In addition, Mr. Siracusa stated that there were no funds to pay for day care and that without day care, the Conservatee could not stay at home. The Court gave the Parties three weeks to reconsider their prespective positions and to come up with solutions for the trust to have sufficient funds to pay for the Conservatee's care. Mr. Siracusa will submit a request to shorten time on the petition for sale of 84 Sylvan. The matter was continued to July 22, 2009 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: David L. Ballati. Reporter: Susan Lee, CSR#4280. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy F. Smith.
